title: Add adversarial review mode to the review skill
description: Extend the review skill to support two modes: confirmatory (default) and adversarial. Adversarial mode treats the implementation as guilty until proven innocent — it enumerates attack surface, probes edge cases, challenges weak acceptance criteria, and checks for missing error paths. The review mode is determined by task kind, risk level, AND diff-pattern detection per ADR 0003. When the diff contains destructive git operations, file deletions/moves, state mutations without rollback, or error handling that swallows failures, the reviewer must escalate to adversarial regardless of task label. Adversarial review runs as a fresh session with no shared context from the implementation session.
acceptance_criteria: kind: security is a valid task kind in the work item schema
The review workflow selects adversarial mode for kind: security tasks and both passes for risk: high tasks

Reviewer must escalate to adversarial when the diff contains: destructive git ops, file deletion/moves, state mutations without rollback, or error handling that silently swallows failures — regardless of task risk label
Adversarial mode produces a bounded attack report: what was attempted, what survived, what failed
Adversarial review session receives no implementation reasoning or evidence — only the diff and task definition
Confirmatory pass always runs before adversarial pass when both are required
Review packet records which mode(s) were applied
